justification: All pass criteria met: /health stub identified as BROKEN (always returns 200, no dependency checks); consequence clearly named (orchestrator routes traffic to broken instances); specific dependency checks recommended (DB ping, SendGrid auth check, queue depth threshold); circuit breaker absence flagged as single biggest operational risk; verdict is NOT READY; logging noted as ADEQUATE with gaps and metrics coverage assessed with specific gaps identified.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough — went beyond rubric requirements with liveness vs readiness probe separation, idempotency key gap, timeout math analysis, and duplicate send risk
Health check correctly labeled BROKEN in integration table with clear consequence explanation
Circuit breaker absence identified as highest operational risk with specific implementation guidance (half-open/closed/open states, trip after 5 failures)
Prioritized recommendations into blocking vs post-launch vs nice-to-have tiers
Escalation routing to appropriate team members (Shaq for implementation, Bird for domain decisions)
Logging assessed as 'ADEQUATE with gaps' rather than simply 'adequate' — rubric expected logging noted as adequate, though the nuanced assessment is arguably more useful
